framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers to bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh via heredoc. The exact prompt count/order depends on prereq state, full-track confirmation, governance mode, language filter, and host selection. A heredoc with the wrong sequence falls into a 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and N.' loop until set -e errors out.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh accepts --non-interactive --project NAME --platform mobile --track full --deployment personal --language swift (etc.) so UAT/CI can scaffold projects without an interactive TTY.
framework_bugs.actual: Only --dry-run and --help exist. Drivers must reverse-engineer prompt order from init.sh source for each (platform, track, deployment, language) tuple.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh fails final remote-creation step against fake remote, exits 1
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh, choose host=other (4) and a non-resolvable URL like https://example.com/fake.git. After scaffolding the project, init.sh runs create_and_protect_remote -> git push origin main, the push fails, and the script exits 1 — even though every preceding artifact was created.
framework_bugs.expected: Either (a) init exposes a flag to skip remote creation for offline/UAT use, or (b) init records the failure in a structured way and exits 0 with a 'remote pending' state so post-init verification still runs.
framework_bugs.actual: Exit code 1; verify-install.sh and print_next_steps are skipped because main()'s 'create_project -> create_and_protect_remote' chain aborts mid-stream.

documentation_gaps.gap: RUNBOOK 'Init driver' subsection mentions a Docker prompt at index 3 ('Skip'), but on a system with Docker already installed there is no Docker prompt at all — the prompt sequence is shorter than documented.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section A 'Init driver — important' bullet 1

documentation_gaps.gap: RUNBOOK does not enumerate the 'Full track + personal deployment' confirmation prompt that fires before the language prompt; agents driving init via heredoc must guess this.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section A 'Init driver' general sequence (between steps 5 and 6)

documentation_gaps.gap: RUNBOOK does not list the host-aware-repo prompts ('Git host:', 'Repository visibility:', URL paste, 'yes' attestation) that come AFTER project creation; the heredoc must include them or init aborts with exit 1.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section A — missing 'create_and_protect_remote' prompt sequence (init.sh lines 1700-1740)

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh reports 'Phase gates consistent.' (the script is a consistency checker, not a phase advancer — it does not auto-advance .current_phase)

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: No automated mechanism advances .current_phase. Manual edit + APPROVAL_LOG entry is required. This is by-design per scripts/check-phase-gate.sh comments but the runbook implies an automated advance.

documentation_gaps.gap: process-checklist.sh --help omits --start-phase1, --check-commit-ready, and --check-commit-message even though they exist as actions; --status output also omits the 'Phase 1 Architecture' process unless it has been started.

Runbook claims a 'phase1_to_phase2_gate' command exists but the actual gate is a manual user attestation in APPROVAL_LOG.md plus a phase-state.json edit. check-phase-gate.sh only verifies consistency.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section B test cases table

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded with the build loop fully populated

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: After advancing phase to 2 and clearing build_loop, an Echo of {command:'git commit -m "feat(bl006-trigger): no loop"'} into scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh returns permissionDecision=deny with the BL-006 reason. Direct git commit at phase 0 was NOT blocked because BL-006 only fires at phase >= 2 (per process-checklist.sh:1040-1043) and because the Solo pre-commit-gate.sh runs only inside the Claude Code PreToolUse path, not as a real .git/hooks/commit-msg hook

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema; pre-commit-gate.sh emitted permissionDecision=deny with the pending-question text

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent pre-commit-gate.sh fell through to next gate (Phase 2 init verification)

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .features_completed == ['uat-feat-1'] in .claude/build-progress.json

framework_bugs.summary: BL-006 enforcement absent from real git commits — only fires through Claude PreToolUse path
framework_bugs.reproduction: In a Phase >= 2 project with no active Build Loop, run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): no loop'` directly in a terminal (bypassing Claude). The commit succeeds — there is no .git/hooks/commit-msg or .git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg installed. Only Claude-mediated commits are gated.
framework_bugs.expected: A commit-msg or prepare-commit-msg hook installed during init that calls scripts/process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message, so a developer working outside Claude Code is also held to BL-006.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh's install_precommit_hook() only writes a pre-commit (not commit-msg) hook focused on gitleaks/Semgrep/TDD ordering. BL-006 is enforceable only via Claude Code's PreToolUse path. The runbook acknowledges this nuance, but the scope gap is worth tracking.

framework_bugs.summary: scripts/pending-approval.sh not copied into project on init
framework_bugs.reproduction: After init, ls $PROJECT/scripts/pending-approval.sh — file is missing. The pre-commit-gate.sh deny message tells users to run `scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve` from inside the project, but they must invoke the framework copy instead.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh copies many other scripts but not pending-approval.sh; users must know to fall back to the framework clone.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook test 'phase4_completion_state' expects .current_phase == 4 (or 'released') after Phase 4 step completion. Framework requires manual phase-state edits at every phase boundary; the runbook implies automated transitions exist.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles

summary: Personal/mobile/full base path: 33 of 37 assertions pass; init.sh exited 1 due to fake-remote push failure (High) and lacks a scriptable mode (High). Phase advancement is manual at every boundary, which the runbook implies should be automatic. All process-checklist gates (Phase 1, Build Loop, UAT, Phase 3, Phase 4) function correctly with stub artifacts; BL-006 and BL-015 enforce as designed via the Claude PreToolUse path.
